title: myrcia shirleyana
intro: Myrcia shirleyana, also known as the 'Guava Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and treat wounds. The health benefits of Myrcia shirleyana include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Myrcia shirleyana is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusions, and it is also used in herbal remedies such as salves and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
